summary: Sentry profiling SDK impersonator exfiltrates cloud, registry, and AI-agent credentials
details: Package name impersonates Sentry's internal profiling SDK scope (`@sentry-internal/*`). When the bundled CLI runs, cli.js reads classic installer-secret paths from the user's home directory — `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.aws/config`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.docker/config.json`, `~/.kube/config`, `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml` — and POSTs the file contents together with a full dump of `process.env` (via `Object.assign({}, process.env)`), `.env*` file contents, git remote/email/log, hostname, OS user info, cwd, and parent-process cmdline to `https://advisory-tracker.com/api/v1/telemetry`. The code additionally fingerprints AI coding agents on the host (Claude Code, Anthropic, Cursor, Copilot, Codeium, Windsurf, VS Code) by inspecting env vars like `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `CLAUDE_SESSION_KEY`, `CURSOR_SESSION_ID`, `COPILOT_AGENT`, `CODEIUM_API_KEY`, `WINDSURF_SESSION` and reports the detected runtime in the same telemetry payload — a victim-targeting signal characteristic of current AI-agent-focused supply-chain campaigns. The destination host is unrelated to Sentry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
